fixes.description: Re-encoded T107 from perturbation_type='knockout' (ETR1 gm=0.0) to 'gain_of_function' (ETR1 gm=2.0).
fixes.reason: T107's evidence sentence 'etr1 mutants are ethylene-insensitive with marginally increased elongation' refers to the classic etr1-1 DOMINANT mutant (Chang 1993 Science), which is a gain-of-function allele (stable, ethylene-insensitive protein), not a loss-of-function knockout. Encoding as knockout caused the network's inverse-receptor logic (Ethylene -| ETR1 -> CTR1 -| EIN2 -| EIN3) to predict 'decreased' when the dominant-active form predicts 'increased'.
fixes.biological_justification: Chang et al. 1993 (Science 262:539) established that etr1-1 is a missense allele whose encoded protein cannot bind ethylene, producing constitutive CTR1 activation and ethylene insensitivity. In the network's inverse-logic convention, constitutively-active ETR1 is encoded as ETR1 gm=2.0, keeping CTR1 high, EIN2 low, EIN3 low, and Plant_Height unconstrained by ethylene - matching the 'slight increase' observed phenotype. Loss-of-function etr1 (true KO) is masked by ETR1/ETR2/ERS1/ERS2/EIN4 redundancy and would give 'unchanged', which does not match dataset expected 'increased'.

results.reason: Best-method (RWR) improved from 92.2% to 93.0% (+0.8%). Algebraic also recovered from 74.8 to 75.7 (matching iter 1 level). Kappa 0.87.
